skill.name: brand-landingpage
skill.description: Guides a focused brand interview, translates answers into Stitch design settings, generates landing page drafts, and packages approved HTML for deployment.
skill.summary: Create a brand-first landing page through interview, Stitch generation, review, and delivery.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity patterns, but review found most were Markdown false positives from backticked terms, color hex values, and examples. The confirmed risks are legitimate workflow actions: installing or using the Stitch SDK, checking an API key environment variable, making external Stitch calls, writing project files, opening local HTML in a browser, and creating a zip bundle.

medium_findings.title: External Stitch SDK and Network Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to verify SDK installation, make a minimal authenticated SDK call, and rely on Stitch documentation and generated download URLs. This is expected for the skill, but it requires third-party network access and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly depends on an external SDK, documentation, authentication check, and generated download URLs. No evidence shows credential exfiltration, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Check for API Key
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to verify that the Stitch API key environment variable is set and to help the user export it if missing. It also instructs the agent not to display or echo the key, which reduces but does not remove secret-handling risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly references checking an API key environment variable and user export flow. The same section includes a protective instruction not to echo the key, so this is a managed medium risk.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes, Browser Opening, and Zip Creation
medium_findings.description: The skill writes state, generated HTML, user-provided assets, design documentation, and deployment bundles. It also opens saved HTML through OS browser commands and creates a zip archive, which are legitimate delivery steps but can affect the local workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflow intentionally creates and copies files, opens local HTML, and runs zip packaging. These actions are scoped to generated project artifacts and do not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command findings come from Markdown inline code, code fences, tool names, enum names, and template examples. They describe the workflow and API shapes rather than executable code embedded in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and examples, not script files or runtime code. The only real command usage is separately captured in the medium findings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Color and Identifier False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts map to color hex values, typography names, state fields, and design-system identifiers. No evidence found of cryptographic operations or weak crypto algorithms being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited context is color mapping and metadata schema content. There is no hashing, encryption, signing, or password-storage logic in these files.

low_findings.title: C2 and SAM Keyword Alerts Are Scanner Noise
low_findings.description: The C2 and Windows SAM alerts appear to be substring matches inside harmless design documentation. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, system database access, or credential dumping intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are color rows or a workflow diagram line. They do not contain code, URLs for control servers, or operating-system credential paths.

content.value_statement: Generic landing pages fail when a product has no clear brand direction. This skill turns a short brand interview into Stitch-generated HTML and a deployable page bundle.
actual_capabilities: Runs a structured brand interview for product purpose, audience, visual tone, colors, typography, and shape preferences.
Maps interview answers to Stitch design-system settings such as color mode, color variant, fonts, and roundness.
Generates desktop and optional mobile landing page drafts through Stitch and opens saved HTML for review.
Guides iterative feedback using targeted edits, variants, rollback, and approval checkpoints.
Creates a delivery bundle with final HTML, design notes, color tokens, user assets, and deployment guidance.
limitations: Requires access to Stitch tools or the Stitch SDK and a configured API key.
Focuses on single-purpose landing pages, not dashboards, documentation sites, or multi-page applications.
Does not directly embed uploaded logos through Stitch generation; assets are saved for later integration.
Generated HTML still needs final links, analytics, real images, and hosting setup before launch.

use_cases.title: Launch a Developer Tool
use_cases.description: Turn a CLI, API, or open source project into a polished landing page with a clear CTA and technical tone.

use_cases.title: Package a SaaS Homepage
use_cases.description: Create a first homepage draft from brand answers, then refine sections, messaging, and visual direction before deployment.

use_cases.title: Translate Brand Preferences
use_cases.description: Convert loose style preferences into concrete colors, fonts, layout direction, and deployable marketing HTML.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with little brand direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page for my product. Interview me first so the brand direction is clear before generation.

prompt_templates.title: Use Existing Product Details
prompt_templates.scenario: User has product context but no design system
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a landing page for this product description. Extract the basics, ask only the missing brand questions, then generate a first draft.

prompt_templates.title: Iterate on a Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the latest generated version and help me refine the page. Focus on message clarity, CTA visibility, brand fit, and section order.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the approved desktop and mobile versions to create the final bundle with design notes, color tokens, assets, and a deployment checklist.

output_examples.input: I need a landing page for a developer API, but I have no brand direction yet.
output_examples.output: A guided interview, a technical brand direction, a Stitch-generated desktop landing page, and a review loop for refinement.

output_examples.input: The draft feels too generic and the CTA is weak.
output: A focused feedback pass on brand fit and CTA hierarchy.
Targeted Stitch edits or variant exploration.
A saved new version for side-by-side review.

output_examples.input: The page is approved. Package it for launch.
output_examples.output: A deployable landing page folder with final HTML, optional mobile HTML, design documentation, design tokens, assets, and deployment guidance.

best_practices: Complete the short brand interview before generating the first page.
Review generated pages in the browser instead of judging from a text summary.
Finalize CTA text, target audience, and brand adjectives before packaging the bundle.
anti_patterns: Skipping the interview and asking for a generic page.
Using the skill for dashboards, component design, or full multi-page applications.
Passing pixel-level CSS instructions when the workflow expects design intent.

faq.question: Does this skill create production-ready code?
faq.answer: It packages deployment-ready HTML, design notes, and tokens. You still need to update links, images, analytics, and hosting settings.

faq.question: Do I need a Stitch account?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow depends on Stitch tools or the Stitch SDK and a configured API key.

faq.question: Can it use my existing logo?
faq.answer: It can save provided assets for the final bundle. Stitch generation is driven by text prompts, so logos may need manual insertion.

faq.question: Can it make mobile layouts?
faq.answer: Yes. After desktop approval, it can generate and review an optional mobile version.

faq.question: Is this useful if I already have a design system?
faq.answer: It is less useful for established design systems. It is best when visual direction is not yet defined.

faq.question: What makes it different from a generic page generator?
faq.answer: It starts with brand discovery, maps answers to design-system choices, and keeps a structured review history.
